fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Alaska CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Alaska - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer CNA license to alaska, alaska CNA endorsement application

board.name: Alaska Board of Nursing
board.mailing_address:
State of Alaska
Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
PO Box 110806
Juneau, AK 99811-0806

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by September 30th of even-numbered years)

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Alaska.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved CNA Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a certified nursing assistant training program approved by the Alaska Board of Nursing. This is a prerequisite for examination and licensure.

steps.title: Pass the CNA Competency Examination
steps.description: Register for and pass both the written and skills portions of the Alaska state-approved CNA competency examination. Passing results must be received by the board prior to certification.

steps.title: Create a MyLicense Account
steps.description: Go to the MyLicense Account Login portal (https://www.commerce.alaska.gov/cbpl/mylicense/) and click "Register Here" to create a new user account with a valid email address.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your MyLicense account, select the "Certified Nursing Assistant" application (Application by Examination for initial licensure or Application by Endorsement for reciprocity), and complete all required sections.

steps.title: Provide Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload any necessary supporting documents directly through the MyLicense portal, such as proof of education completion, and authorize a criminal background check (if required). The board will receive exam results directly from the testing vendor.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee online via credit card within the MyLicense system to finalize your application.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Alaska CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $200. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Alaska.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in AK.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Alaska.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Alaska
hero.dek: To apply for a CNA license in Alaska, candidates must register and apply through the MyLicense Account Login portal managed by the Alaska Board of Nursing.
